fields.description:
## Why

Blocking is project-owned policy; rebar's job is to surface the same eval/calibration instruments
it uses on its built-ins so a maintainer can decide informed. Today `eval_solver.run_case` is a
**closed hardcoded dispatch** (only `completion-verifier` / `ticket-quality` / `spec-alignment`;
raises otherwise) and the CLI is validate-only ‚Äî so a project criterion cannot be run through the
eval harness at all.

## What

- A criterion-aware runner extending `eval_solver.run_case` so an arbitrary (built-in or project)
  criterion prompt_id is runnable ‚Äî reconcile the invocation model (the criterion runs as its
  Pass-1 finder against a fixture); resolve the T3 advisory (the existing solver's invocation model
  vs a per-criterion prompt).
- `rebar criteria eval <id>` ‚Äî a live CLI (not validate-only) that runs the criterion's
  must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures from `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` (discovery
  already exists) with N-run stability.
- A calibration view over the existing Œ∫/parity primitives: per criterion print **recall**
  (=fraction of must-fire fixtures it fires on), **false-accept** (=fraction of must-not-fire it
  wrongly fires on), **Cohen's Œ∫** and **agreement %** (verdict vs the fixture's labeled expect).
  Reconcile the preview/eval `verdict` vocabulary with the fixture `expect` vocabulary.

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py` (criterion-aware dispatch), `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` /
  `eval_scorers.py` (reuse discrimination scorers, Œ∫, parity), `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`
  (`rebar criteria eval`), calibration rendering.
- Depends on story `#1` (a criterion must be loadable/activatable to eval it).
- Out: REVIEW_RESULT FP-replay (F1) ‚Äî gate-agnostic, deferred; project criteria inherit it later.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `eval_solver.run_case` runs an arbitrary criterion prompt_id (built-in or project), not the
      closed 3-id set; the invocation model is documented.
- [ ] `rebar criteria eval <id>` runs must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures live from
      `.rebar/evals/` with N-run stability.
- [ ] The calibration view prints recall, false-accept, Cohen's Œ∫, agreement % per criterion, with
      each metric defined; the eval `verdict` vocabulary is reconciled with the fixture `expect`.
- [ ] FP-replay (F1) is explicitly out of scope.


## Invocation model & implementation contract (remediation, run 1)

### Criterion arm of `run_case` (clears G6/T1/T8 blocker)
The existing `run_case` branches (`case_store` + a disposable git repo + `create_ticket`) are the
AGENTIC store-reader ops ‚Äî structurally inapplicable to a plan-review criterion, which is a
single-turn/agentic **finder over inline text**. The criterion arm therefore does NOT use
`case_store`. Concrete path:
- `run_case(prompt_id, case, *, runner, graph=False, repo_root=None)` gains a `repo_root=None`
  param (default `config.repo_root()`; back-compat for existing callers). If `prompt_id` resolves to
  a criterion ‚Äî bare id or `plan-review-<id>`, present in `registry.by_id(repo_root)` ‚Äî take the
  criterion arm; else the existing 3-id dispatch (unknown non-criterion still raises `ValueError`).
- The arm: `desc = registry.by_id(repo_root)[cid]`; `cfg = resolve_gate_config(repo_root)`;
  `findings = passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=case["input"], chunk=[desc],
  agentic=registry.exec_tier(desc)=="AGENT")`; return `{"findings": findings}`. No store, no ticket.
  (Container G3/G4 + ISF need a graph/log, not inline text ‚Äî OUT of scope here; a criterion whose
  fixtures require them errors with a clear message.)

### Verdict ‚Üî expect vocabulary reconciliation (clears T8/AC3)
`_fired(verdict) = len(verdict.get("findings") or []) > 0`. A case's expected fire is derived from
its `expect` via the EXISTING sets: `FIRE_EXPECTS = {"finding","fail"}` ‚Üí expected-fire;
`NOFIRE_EXPECTS = {"pass"}` ‚Üí expected-no-fire. Only fire/no-fire cases feed the calibration
(validity/impact/novelty axis cases are skipped, not miscounted). Two-category labels `"fire"` /
`"no_fire"` feed `cohens_kappa` + agreement.

### Calibration metrics (clears AC3 metric defs)
`calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runner=None, runs=1, solve=None)` in eval.py:
- `recall` = fired / total must-fire fixtures; `false_accept` = wrongly-fired / total must-not-fire
  fixtures; `agreement` = fraction of cases where observed == expected; `kappa` =
  `cohens_kappa(expected_labels, observed_labels)`.
- Injectable `solve` (mirrors `run_eval`) so tests compute metrics deterministically without a model.

### N-run stability (clears E2/T5e/G6 ‚Äî N is configurable)
`runs` (CLI `--runs`, default **1**) is N. Each case runs N times; its observed verdict is the
MAJORITY fire/no-fire across the N runs, and per-case `stability` = fraction of the N runs agreeing
with that majority (1.0 = fully stable). The view prints the min/mean stability so a flaky criterion
is visible. N=1 ‚áí stability trivially 1.0.

### CLI error states + sequencing (clears T6/E5/G5)
`rebar criteria eval <id>` errors clearly (non-zero exit, no traceback) on: missing/empty id; a
criterion id absent from the registry (`unknown criterion`); an absent `.rebar/evals/<prompt_id>.eval.yaml`
fixture (`no eval fixtures for <id>`); a fixture with zero fire/no-fire cases (`empty calibration
dataset`). Thin-slice sequencing: (1) `run_case` criterion arm + a unit test with a fake runner;
(2) `calibrate_criterion` + a deterministic-`solve` test; (3) CLI wiring + error-path tests.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)

- [ ] The criterion arm of `run_case` calls `passes.pass1_chunk` directly (no `case_store`), with a
      documented `repo_root` param; a fake-runner unit test asserts fire (non-empty findings) and
      no-fire (empty) map correctly.
- [ ] `calibrate_criterion` computes recall / false-accept / Œ∫ / agreement / stability with an
      injectable `solve`; a deterministic test asserts the metric math (no live model).
- [ ] `rebar criteria eval <id>` errors clearly (non-zero, no traceback) on unknown id / absent
      fixture / empty dataset; each has a test.
